title: abutilon indicum var. forrestii
intro: Abutilon indicum var. forrestii, also known as Indian mallow, is a variant of the Abutilon indicum plant that is native to the Himalayan region. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Abutilon indicum var. forrestii include the treatment of various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or the extraction of its roots and leaves, which are used to create teas, tonics, or topical creams.
